title: Who won Tournament of Champions Season 7 (Food Network)?
rules: If Adam Greenberg is officially declared the winner of Tournament of Champions Season 7 before Dec 31, 2026, then the market resolves to Yes.
tavily_brief:
[redacted] The competition features 32 chefs competing in a single-elimination bracket format for a $150,000 prize and the championship belt. The season's major twist involves four mystery "culinary icons" seeded as top competitors, revealed only upon entering the arena. Adam Greenberg, one of the 20 pre-announced competitors, is a Connecticut-based chef and owner of Sparrow Pizza Bar with a strong Food Network track record. According to pre-competition interviews (PRIMETIMER, We-Ha), Greenberg holds a 4-0 record on Chopped, won [redacted] He expressed humility about competing alongside "living legends" and stated he "never says no" to Food Network challenges. [redacted] He faced Kevin Lee in a head-to-head battle where Lee scored 90 points to Greenberg's 80 points, resulting in Greenberg's elimination. Greenberg himself acknowledged the loss, telling Lee "Kicked my a*s—good job, man!"

The competition progressed through multiple rounds with Bryan Voltaggio emerging as a strong contender, eventually reaching the finals. The April 19 finale episode determined the ultimate champion, with Bryan Voltaggio ultimately winning the tournament with the highest score of the season (94 out of 100 points) in the championship battle against Kevin Lee. [redacted]

args.reasoning:
Evidence inventory (strong): Food Network’s official episodic recap page, titled “TOC VII: Weekly Updates, Eliminations and Who Took Home the Belt” (published Mar 23, 2026 on foodnetwork.com), explicitly states that Bryan Voltaggio won Tournament of Champions VII, defeating Kevin Lee by a single point in the finale to claim the $150,000 prize and the belt. This is a first-party source from the broadcaster and names the runner-up, margin, and prize, which directly resolves the question of the winner. 

Implication: Because the show’s own site unambiguously names Bryan Voltaggio as the TOC VII champion and specifies the final matchup (vs. Kevin Lee) and one-point margin, the probability that Bryan is the winner is overwhelmingly high; the probability that any other listed chef (including Adam Greenberg) is the winner is correspondingly negligible. 

Moderate corroboration: Press and social coverage following the finale align with Food Network’s recap, consistently naming Bryan Voltaggio as champion and Kevin Lee as runner-up. While secondary sources are not necessary when the first-party site already resolves it, their alignment reduces the likelihood of a reporting error. 

Upset check: There is no evidence of a post-finale reversal, controversy, or correction; the official page presents a settled outcome. 

Sanity: TOC finales are definitive on-air competitions; winners are not typically altered after broadcast. First-party confirmation with specific final details (opponent, score, prize) strongly indicates resolution.

Therefore, assign near-total probability to Bryan Voltaggio as the winner, with only minimal residual allocated across the remaining names to reflect the tool’s probability bounds constraint.
